headings: BROWNSTONE CONSTRUCTION, LTD. SUBCONTRACT FOR Allegra at Shreveport
text: Section  4. Subcontractor  expressly  agrees  and  understands  that  time  is  of  the  essence  in  performing  this Subcontract. In line with that understanding, Subcontractor agrees to commence the Subcontract Work on and no later than Forty-eight (48) hours after Contractor's notice to proceed. Subcontractor shall perform the Subcontract Work in accordance with the Contractor's Project schedule, and agrees to complete the Subcontract Work within the original Contract time plus any extension of Contract time allowed by the Owner. The Subcontractor shall keep itself  thoroughly informed as to the progress of the Project; begin work upon notification by the Contractor and prosecute the work continuously and uninterruptedly with all possible speed. The Subcontractor, however, shall not be held responsible for any delays caused exclusively by the neglect of the Contractor. The Subcontractor agrees to prosecute the Subcontract Work, and the several parts thereof, at such times and in such order as the Contractor considers necessary to keep the same sufficiently in advance of the other parts of the building and to avoid any delay in the completion of the Project as a whole. Subcontractor is unconditionally obligated to provide sufficient materials, equipment and a labor force consisting of a sufficient number of properly skilled workers to, at all times, satisfy  Project  needs  and  conform  to  the  Contractor's  Project  schedule  applicable  to  the  Subcontract  Work. Contractor  has  absolute  authority  to  require  Subcontractor  to  supplement  its  labor  force,  work  overtime,  or otherwise accelerate Subcontractor's performance to the extent deemed necessary by Contractor to ensure that the Subcontract Work progresses in accordance with the Project schedule. If Subcontractor fails to provide sufficient materials, equipment and/or a labor force consisting of a sufficient number of properly skilled workers to satisfy Project needs and conform to the Contractor's Project schedule applicable to the Subcontract Work ('Failure'), Contractor shall provide Subcontractor with written notice ('Notice') of the Failure and, after receiving  Notice,  Subcontractor  shall  have  forty-eight  (48)  hours  to  cure  the  Failure.  In  the  event  that Subcontractor does not cure the Failure within forty-eight (48) hours after receiving Notice, Contractor shall have the uncontestable right to assess Subcontractor and offset against any funds due Subcontractor under this Subcontract or any other agreement $250.00 per day as liquidated damages, which liquidated damages shall cease to accrue only after Subcontractor is in compliance with the Contractor's Project schedule and otherwise  cures  the  Failure  to  Contractor's  satisfaction.  Contractor  and  Subcontractor  agree  that  such liquidated damages are a reasonable estimate of Contractor's actual damages as a result of any Failure, the amount  of  which  actual  damages  would  be  difficult  to  ascertain,  and  such  liquidated  damages  are  not  a penalty. Additionally, the Subcontractor shall reimburse the Contractor for any loss, damage, or extra expense paid or  incurred  by  the  Contractor,  including  but  not  limited  to  liquidated  damages  assessed  against  Contractor  by Owner, which are due to Subcontractor's failure to deliver any and all materials as required, or to properly perform any and all work in keeping with the progress of the Contractor's Project schedule, or to properly perform any term, covenant or condition contained in this Subcontract, or which is due to the breach of any of the provisions of this Subcontract. It is further agreed that if the Subcontractor fails or refuses to proceed with the Subcontract Work as directed by the Contractor, or fails to perform said Subcontract Work in accordance herewith, in whole or in part, or fails  to  perform  any  term,  covenant  or  condition  contained  in  his  Subcontract,  the  Contractor  may  upon  written notice to the Subcontractor's last known address take any steps that Contractor deems advisable to secure necessary labor or materials by contract or otherwise, and may take over all or any part of the Subcontractor's equipment, materials,  etc.,  and  may  prosecute  the  Subcontract  Work  to  completion.  In  the  event  that  Contractor  deems  this procedure  necessary  for  proper  conduct  of  the  Subcontract  Work,  all  monies  expended  therefor  and  damages incurred as a result of Subcontractor's default shall be deducted from the Subcontract Sum herein stated, and if such expenditures exceed the amount otherwise due to the Subcontractor hereunder, the Subcontractor agrees to pay to the Contractor on demand the full amount of such excess, together with interest thereon at the rate of six percent per annum, or the maximum rate allowed by law, whichever is less, until fully paid, and Contractor shall have the right to offset such amounts against payments then or thereafter due Subcontractor under this Subcontract or any other agreement.
